column1: Listen, I need the exact legal framework governing copyright in press releases—no vague generalities. Break it down by jurisdiction, citing Title 17 U.S.C. § 102, the Berne Convention’s Article 2(1), and any relevant case law like *Feist Publications v. Rural Telephone Service*. What’s the threshold for originality in a press release, and how does fair use (17 U.S.C. § 107) apply to corporate announcements? Don’t waste my time with basic summaries; give me the granular, actionable specifics—now.

Suppose I'm working on a documentary about the history of jazz and I want to include clips of early recordings—what would be the legal difference between using the sheet music of an older composition versus using an actual vintage recording of that same piece?
